title: First Beach Sampling Trial
text: This entry documents the first field sampling to test tidal and environmental effects on foraminifera.

title: First Beach Sampling Trial
text:
Objective
Test the initial beach sampling timing during outgoing tide.

title: First Beach Sampling Trial
text:
Method

Sampled at 0 hr, 1 hr, 2 hr, 3 hr

Collected sediment + water samples

Preserved some samples in glyoxal for fixation testing

title: First Beach Sampling Trial
text:
Observations

Salinity varied more than expected

Sediment composition changed across locations

Some shells showed minor breakage

title: First Beach Sampling Trial
text:
Next Steps

Standardize sampling location

Begin glyoxal fixation trials

Capture microscope images of morphospecies

title: Lab Notebook
text:
This is an open record of ongoing research exploring how foraminifera respond to environmental stress in natural marine systems. Entries document experimental design, field sampling, protocol development, and early-stage results.
Rather than only presenting final conclusions, this notebook captures the process of science in real time—including challenges, adjustments, and unexpected observations.

title: Lab Notebook
text:
This is an open record of ongoing research exploring how foraminifera respond to environmental stress in natural marine systems. Entries document experimental design, field sampling, protocol development, and early-stage results.
Rather than only presenting final conclusions, this notebook captures the process of science in real time—including challenges, adjustments, and unexpected observations.

title: Lab Notebook
text:
What This Notebook Tracks

Field sampling across tidal cycles and environmental conditions

Development of in situ glyoxal fixation protocols

Microscopy and morphospecies identification

RNA extraction and sequencing preparation

Data exploration and early interpretations

title: Lab Notebook
text:
How to Use This Page

Search to find specific experiments, methods, or observations

Filter by category (e.g., sampling, microscopy, RNA-seq)

Sort entries by date or topic

Browse chronologically to follow the progression of the project

title: Lab Notebook
text:
Current Project Focus
The current phase of research is centered on:

Optimizing glyoxal fixation for field conditions

Comparing in situ vs lab-preserved samples

Capturing short-term biological responses across tidal changes

title: Lab Notebook
text:
Why an Open Lab Notebook?
Maintaining an open notebook increases transparency and reproducibility in scientific research. It also provides a clearer picture of how knowledge is built—from initial questions to refined methods and final results.

title: Ethan Doan
text: Exploring how marine organisms respond to environmental change

title: Ethan Doan
text:
About Me
Hi, I’m Ethan. I’m a student at William & Mary studying science, with a strong interest in biochemistry and how it connects to real-world problems.
I’m especially interested in how biological systems respond to real-world environmental change, particularly at the molecular level.
Outside of academics, I’m always looking for ways to learn, improve, and take on new challenges.

title: Ethan Doan
section: Research: Foram Response to Environmental Stress
text:
Research: Foram Response to Environmental Stress

Overview
This project explores how foraminifera—microscopic marine organisms critical to ocean ecosystems—respond to environmental stress such as changes in temperature and salinity.


Methodology

Field sampling across tidal cycles

Hourly sampling over ~3-hour windows

Glyoxal fixation (lab vs in situ)

Microscopy + morphospecies identification

RNA sequencing + PCR



Timeline

Weeks 1–3: Sampling

Weeks 1–5: Fixation testing

Weeks 2–5: RNA prep

Weeks 6–7: Sequencing



Why It Matters
This work improves how scientists study organisms in real environments and strengthens foraminifera as indicators of ocean health.

title: Ethan Doan
text:
Lab Notebook

Week 1: First sampling trip — testing collection timing

Week 2: Glyoxal pH trials

Week 3: Microscopy + species ID

Week 4: RNA extraction + PCR

(Updated regularly as the project progresses)

title: Ethan Doan

HPC data analysis
